questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Former mining regions are being revitalised through:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She lost not only her wallet but also.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Which of the following was a social cost of the Industrial Revolution?
options: Poor working conditions and child labour
questions.solution: Poor working conditions and child labour

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering with high fever.
options: He is suffering from high fever.
He is suffering of high fever.
He is suffering by high fever.
He is suffering with high fever.
questions.solution: He is suffering from high fever.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's solar power capacity in 2024 is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: No sooner he arrived than the meeting started.
options: No sooner did he arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he had arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he arrives than the meeting started.
No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.
questions.solution: No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

What powered steam engines during the Industrial Revolution?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
options: The professor gave a one hour lecture.
The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
questions.solution: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

Coal accounts for about what percentage of India's installed capacity?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She lost not only her wallet but also.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The word 'mandatory' in the passage most nearly means:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Hardly had he arrived than the meeting started.
options: He had hardly arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived then the meeting started.
Hardly he had arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.
questions.solution: Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Washeries are used to:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
options: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
A pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pairs of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
questions.solution: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a risk in underground mining?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
options: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.
He is one who has excelled in the field.
He is one of those who excelled in the field.
He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
questions.solution: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

Which of the following is mentioned as a concern related to coal usage?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the p–V diagram shown, two expansions start from the same state (V₁ ≈ 1). The steeper curve (1) is the:
questions.solution: Adiabatic curves (pV^γ) are steeper than isothermal curves (pV) on a p–V diagram because γ > 1.

figure.caption: p–V diagram: adiabatic vs isothermal

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gear tooth bending — Lewis equation:
options: σ = F_t/(b·m·Y). Y = Lewis form factor (based on number of teeth)
questions.solution: Lewis: tooth bending stress. Y: more teeth = higher Y. Velocity factor for dynamic load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Subcooling in the condenser:
options: Increases the refrigeration effect (cooling below saturation)
questions.solution: Subcooling: liquid cooled below saturation temp before expansion. Increases RE, improves COP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about pipe flow is INCORRECT?
options: Doubling the mean velocity halves the frictional head loss in turbulent flow
Laminar flow has a parabolic velocity profile
Turbulent head loss roughly varies with velocity squared
questions.solution: In turbulent flow head loss ∝ V², so doubling V roughly quadruples it — the statement is wrong.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Clausius inequality states:
questions.solution: Clausius: ∮ δQ/T ≤ 0 for any cycle (equality if reversible).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The gating system includes:
options: Sprue, runner, gate, riser (feeds molten metal into mold)
questions.solution: Gating: controls metal flow. Riser: feeds shrinkage (open or blind). Chills: accelerate cooling.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Grain refinement strengthening mechanism:
options: Increases strength by reducing grain size

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inversion of a mechanism means:
questions.solution: Inversion: fixing a different link in a kinematic chain.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Center of gravity of composite body:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A long, slender column under axial compression is most likely to fail by:
questions.solution: Slender columns fail by elastic buckling (Euler) well before the crushing stress is reached.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Refrigeration — vapor compression:
options: Compressor → condenser → expansion valve → evaporator. COP = Q_L/W_comp
questions.solution: VCR: most common refrigeration. COP = (h₁-h₄)/(h₂-h₁). Subcooling + superheating improve COP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tool life — Taylor's equation:
options: VT^n = C. V = cutting speed, T = tool life, n = exponent, C = constant
questions.solution: n: 0.1-0.2 (HSS), 0.2-0.4 (carbide), 0.4-0.7 (ceramic). Higher n = better speed tolerance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The purpose of annealing is to:
questions.solution: Annealing: soften, relieve stresses, improve machinability.

questions.stem: Moment of inertia of a solid cylinder about its central axis is:

questions.stem: A beam of span 5 m has a UDL of 15 kN/m. The max shear force is:

questions.stem: Wall k=15 W/m·K, area=0.05 m², thickness=0.2m, ΔT=100°C. Conduction heat transfer Q (W) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The dew point temperature is the temperature at which:
questions.solution: Dew point: saturation temperature, RH = 100%, condensation starts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Affinity laws for pumps:
options: Q ∝ N, H ∝ N², P ∝ N³. Speed change: Q₂/Q₁ = N₂/N₁
questions.solution: Affinity: for same impeller diameter. Diameter change: Q ∝ D³, H ∝ D², P ∝ D⁵.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Endurance limit of steel:
options: The stress below which it can withstand infinite cycles (≈ 0.5 × S_ut)
questions.solution: Endurance limit: S_e ≈ 0.5 S_ut for steel (up to ~10⁶ cycles). No endurance limit for Al, Mg.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Epicyclic gear train has:
options: Gears whose axes move (planet gears rotate around sun gear)
questions.solution: Epicyclic: sun, planet carrier, ring gear. Used in automatic transmissions, differentials.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Factor of safety:
options: FOS = Ultimate strength / Allowable stress. Or Yield strength / Working stress
questions.solution: FOS > 1. Depends on material, loading, consequences of failure, reliability.

options: V = πDN/1000 m/min. f = mm/rev. d = depth (mm). MRR = V·f·d
questions.solution: MRR: material removal rate. V: higher → tool wear. f: higher → surface finish worse.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Governor sensitivity:
options: Ratio of change in equilibrium speed to change in sleeve lift
questions.solution: Sensitivity = dN/dh. More sensitive = smaller speed change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam 3m span carries UDL 20 kN/m. E = 200 GPa, section 40×60mm. Maximum deflection (mm) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cam-and-follower mechanism is used to obtain a:
options: Prescribed follower motion from a rotating cam
questions.solution: The cam profile dictates the desired displacement–time motion of the follower.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rectangular beam 50mm × 80mm. Moment of inertia I:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A universal joint:
options: Transmits power between shafts with angular misalignment
questions.solution: Hooke's joint: ω₂/ω₁ = cosθ/(1 - sin²θ·cos²φ). Non-constant velocity (single joint). Double = constant velocity.

questions.stem: Slenderness ratio:
options: λ = L_eff / r_min. r_min = √(I_min/A). Short (λ<30), intermediate (30<λ<120), long (λ>120)
questions.solution: Long columns: Euler buckling. Intermediate: Johnson formula. Short: crushing failure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a heat exchanger, LMTD stands for the log-mean of the:
options: Temperature differences at the two ends
questions.solution: LMTD averages the end temperature differences for the heat-transfer rate Q = UA·LMTD.
